impacts.statement: this is the record level reached on April 30, 2017. Portions of Highway 106 flood near the gage site.
this is the flood level which occurred on November 14th 1993.
water reaches the river gage and bottom of the Highway 106 bridge. Buildings near the gage site including Harvey's Canoe rental become inundated with several feet of water.
major flooding occurs at the gage site. Lower portions of Highway 106 flood.
the Alley Spring Campground is completely flooded.
moderate flooding begins at the gage site.  Water inundates the entire picnic area one quarter mile downstream from the gage site.
much of Alley Spring Campground floods. Water flows through Alley Spring Campground and isolates the 200 Area Camp Sites.
water impacts Alley Spring Campground. Water is one foot in depth at the 600, 800 and 900 loop camp sites.
water begins to impact the 600, 800 and 900 loop sites of Alley Spring Campground one quarter mile upstream from the gage site.
floating access to the Jacks Fork at Alley Spring is closed.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.
